questions.text: In Kerala, a fisherman joins a 'Chit Fund' (chitty) where 20 members pay ₹2,000/month. The monthly pool of ₹40,000 is given to one member by lottery. If you win in month 3, how much profit do you make (ignoring any discount at auction)?
questions.explanation: You paid: 3 months × ₹2,000 = ₹6,000. You receive: ₹40,000. Net = 40,000 - 6,000 = ₹34,000 profit (you still pay remaining 17 months but that's the total cost). Chitty funds are regulated by Kerala Chit Fund Act. Arithmetic progressions model the financial outcomes!
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.text: A Kerala NRI sends ₹1,00,000 home. The money is invested in a business giving 15% profit in year 1, which reduces by 2% each year (AP). What is the total profit over 5 years?
questions.explanation: Profit rates: 15%, 13%, 11%, 9%, 7% (AP with a=15, d=-2). Sum = 5/2 × (2×15 + 4×(-2)) = 5/2 × (30-8) = 5/2 × 22 = 55%. Total profit = 55% of 1,00,000 = ₹55,000. NRI remittances to Kerala total $16 billion/year — managing this money requires exactly these calculations.
questions.subject: Mathematics
